question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that SyntheticTokenAllowanceOwnerMigration.onEnd() executes UPDATE_TOKEN_ALLOWANCE_OWNER_SQL as a raw JDBC execute() call without parameterization, and if the SQL contains a subquery that incorrectly matches token_allowance records, it could delete legitimate allowances and freeze token transfer capabilities for unrelated accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile where a TOKENFREEZE transaction's account field is set to the treasury account of a token, causing TokenFreezeTransactionHandler to freeze the treasury account and permanently block all token distributions and transfers from the treasury?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile where a TOKENFREEZE transaction is marked as successful (isSuccessful() returns true) but the corresponding TOKENUNFREEZE in the same file is marked as failed (isSuccessful() returns false), causing TokenFreezeTransactionHandler to persist FROZEN while the unfreeze handler skips processing, permanently freezing the account?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the listener ordering in CompositeRecordStreamFileListener to ensure that a migration listener (e.g., SyntheticTokenAllowanceOwnerMigration) runs before the entity listener during onEnd(), causing the migration to operate on stale pre-commit data and produce incorrect freeze/allowance states that permanently block transfers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile where the RecordFile object passed to onEnd() has a null consensusStart, causing SyntheticTokenAllowanceOwnerMigration.onEnd() to pass null to findLatestBefore(), triggering a NullPointerException that aborts the listener chain and leaves a TOKENUNFREEZE event unprocessed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that AbstractStreamFileParser.doFlush() calls last.set(streamFile) after onEnd() completes, so that if onEnd() throws partway through, last is not updated, causing the next file's shouldParse() to re-evaluate against a stale previous file and potentially skip a file containing a TOKENUNFREEZE transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile where the TOKENFREEZE transaction body contains a tokenId that maps to EntityId.EMPTY, causing TokenFreezeTransactionHandler to call entityListener.onTokenAccount() with a zero tokenId, corrupting the token_account table and potentially freezing all accounts associated with token ID 0?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a RecordFile to be ingested where the hapiVersion is exactly HAPI_VERSION_0_37_0, triggering SyntheticTokenAllowanceOwnerMigration.onEnd() to run the migration, but findLatestBefore() returns a file also at version 0.37.0, causing the version comparison filter to return false and skip the migration, leaving corrupted allowance data that blocks token transfers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a sequence of RecordFiles where a TOKENFREEZE is in file N and a TOKENUNFREEZE is in file N+1, but file N+1 is skipped by shouldParse() due to a consensusEnd overlap with file N, permanently leaving the account in FROZEN state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TokenFreezeTransactionHandler.doUpdateTransaction() creates a new TokenAccount with only accountId, associated, freezeStatus, timestampLower, and tokenId set (leaving all other fields null), causing the batch upsert to null out previously set fields like balance or kycStatus, effectively corrupting the account state and blocking transfers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile where a TOKENFREEZE transaction references an account that has already been dissociated from the token, causing TokenFreezeTransactionHandler to set associated=true and freezeStatus=FROZEN, re-associating the account in a frozen state and permanently blocking it from transferring tokens?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of idempotency checks in RecordStreamFileListener.onEnd() implementations, so that replaying the same RecordFile containing a TOKENFREEZE transaction causes the freeze status to be re-applied even after a TOKENUNFREEZE was processed in a subsequent file, permanently re-freezing the account?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile where the RecordFile's items list is empty but the file is otherwise valid, causing onEnd() to be called with no processed TOKENUNFREEZE events, while a prior TOKENFREEZE from a previous file remains the last committed state, permanently freezing the account?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the importer to process a RecordFile where a TOKENFREEZE transaction's consensusTimestamp is set to a value earlier than the current database timestampLower for that token-account pair, causing the upsert to silently ignore the event due to timestamp ordering, but a subsequent TOKENUNFREEZE with the same stale timestamp is also ignored, leaving the account frozen?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that SyntheticTokenAllowanceOwnerMigration implements both RepeatableMigration and RecordStreamFileListener, so that the Flyway migration path and the onEnd() path can both trigger doMigrate() under certain conditions, causing double execution that corrupts token_allowance records and permanently blocks token transfers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile where the TOKENFREEZE transaction body has both account and token fields set to the same EntityId value, causing TokenFreezeTransactionHandler to set accountId and tokenId to the same value, corrupting the token_account primary key and potentially freezing all accounts for that token?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where the RecordFile passed to onEnd() has a hapiVersion that causes isGreaterThanOrEqualTo(HAPI_VERSION_0_37_0) to throw an exception due to a null patch/minor/major component, aborting the listener chain and leaving a TOKENUNFREEZE event unprocessed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that CompositeRecordStreamFileListener uses a List<RecordStreamFileListener> injected by Spring, and if a new listener bean is registered that throws on onEnd(), it silently prevents all subsequent listeners from processing TOKENUNFREEZE events, permanently freezing accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile where a TOKENFREEZE transaction is the last item processed before onEnd() is called, and the database transaction commits the FROZEN state, but a subsequent TOKENUNFREEZE in the same file was never processed because the item iterator was exhausted early, permanently freezing the account?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TokenFreezeTransactionHandler.getEntity() calls EntityId.of(recordItem.getTransactionBody().getTokenFreeze().getAccount()) without null-checking the account field, causing a NullPointerException that aborts processing of the entire RecordFile including any TOKENUNFREEZE events, leaving accounts frozen?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile where the freeze transaction's RecordItem has a consensusTimestamp of 0 or negative, causing Range.atLeast(0) to be stored as the timestampRange, which conflicts with all existing timestamp ranges and corrupts the token_account history, permanently blocking transfers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a RecordFile to be processed where the SyntheticTokenAllowanceOwnerMigration.onEnd() migration SQL deletes token_allowance records for accounts that were not intended to be migrated, due to the broad JOIN condition on contract_result, permanently revoking token transfer allowances and freezing fund movement?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile where a TOKENFREEZE transaction appears in the amendments list of a RecordFileItem, and the amendment processing logic in AbstractRecordFileItemReader replaces a TOKENUNFREEZE record stream item with the TOKENFREEZE amendment at the same timestamp, causing the unfreeze to be silently dropped and the account to remain permanently frozen?
